title: Raise retry cap from 2 to 3 for YCE build failures
description: 13 distinct ideas in this window hit `max_retries_exceeded` at exactly 2 retries; uniform pattern suggests ceiling effect, not idea-specific failure. YCE build failures (265-r1, 277-r1, 285, 282) show `-r1` suffix indicating retry path is already exercised.
suggested_change: Bump Metroplex retry cap from 2 → 3 for `yce_build_failed` outcomes. Keep cap at 2 for `review_failed` (those are deterministic quality gate failures where retry is unlikely to help).
impact: If even 25% of the 13 capped ideas succeed on a 3rd retry, that's ~3 additional published builds per cycle — meaningful against a baseline of 10 published this period.

evidence.description: 13 distinct ideas in this window hit `max_retries_exceeded` at exactly 2 retries; uniform pattern suggests ceiling effect, not idea-specific failure. YCE build failures (265-r1, 277-r1, 285, 282) show `-r1` suffix indicating retry path is already exercised.

title: Lower deferral cap from 10 to 5 and trigger earlier rejection
description: 56/90 (62%) of build failures are `max_deferrals_reached (10)`. With 8,453 deferrals on 8,854 triage decisions (95.5%), ideas are cycling through deferral without meaningful re-evaluation. Deferring 10 times before rejecting wastes triage cycles.
suggested_change: Reduce `MAX_DEFERRALS` from 10 → 5 in Metroplex triage config. After 5 deferrals, auto-reject with reason `deferral_cap_reached` instead of continuing to cycle.
impact: Reduces triage cycle waste by ~50% on deferred ideas, freeing cycles for fresh signals. Should not reduce published count since `max_deferrals_reached` ideas are already not publishing.

evidence.description: 56/90 (62%) of build failures are `max_deferrals_reached (10)`. With 8,453 deferrals on 8,854 triage decisions (95.5%), ideas are cycling through deferral without meaningful re-evaluation. Deferring 10 times before rejecting wastes triage cycles.

title: Set quality threshold to 52, not the suggested 49.8
description: Suggested threshold of 49.8 is the midpoint between published avg (58.3) and failed avg (41.3), but score distribution is compressed (completed range 45–62). At 49.8, ~9 of 43 published builds (those scoring 36–49) would have been blocked — rejecting known-good output. A threshold of 52 sits 1σ below published mean while still filtering all Tyrest-rejected (avg 38.3, max 56) except the 2 borderline outliers.
suggested_change: Override the midpoint-formula suggestion; set quality threshold to 52. Log the override reason so the ratchet doesn't silently tighten back to 49.8 on the next run.
impact: Preserves the current published throughput while tightening against the long tail of low-scoring rejections. Avoids ratchet whiplash.

evidence.description: Suggested threshold of 49.8 is the midpoint between published avg (58.3) and failed avg (41.3), but score distribution is compressed (completed range 45–62). At 49.8, ~9 of 43 published builds (those scoring 36–49) would have been blocked — rejecting known-good output. A threshold of 52 sits 1σ below published mean while still filtering all Tyrest-rejected (avg 38.3, max 56) except the 2 borderline outliers.

title: Add YCE build-environment health check before dispatch
description: 4 YCE build failures this window (265-r1, 277-r1, 285, 282) clustered in time, suggesting environment regression (template, toolchain, or worktree state) rather than idea-specific failure. Friction notes corroborate: "Kup's restart script had hardcoded worktree path that Claude caught and fixed before merge" and "first retro-run had ~55% failure rate due to git fetch-not-pull, duplicates".
suggested_change: Add a pre-build YCE smoke test (hello-world build on a fixed reference idea) that runs before each dispatch batch. If the smoke test fails, pause dispatch and alert rather than cascading failures across multiple ideas.
impact: Prevents the "cluster of 4 failures" pattern; fail-fast on environment regression saves retry-cap budget for legitimate idea-level failures.

evidence.description: 4 YCE build failures this window (265-r1, 277-r1, 285, 282) clustered in time, suggesting environment regression (template, toolchain, or worktree state) rather than idea-specific failure. Friction notes corroborate: "Kup's restart script had hardcoded worktree path that Claude caught and fixed before merge" and "first retro-run had ~55% failure rate due to git fetch-not-pull, duplicates".

title: Tighten triage approve-rate via batched scoring, not per-item deferral
description: 3.4% approve rate (300/8854) with 95.5% deferral rate suggests triage is risk-averse to approval but unwilling to outright reject. The per-item scoring doesn't use relative context — an idea of score 55 might be top-quartile this week but deferred anyway.
suggested_change: Modify Galvatron triage to score ideas in batches of N (e.g., 20), then approve top 25% / reject bottom 50% / defer middle 25% — with deferral counter. Creates forcing function against indefinite deferral.
impact: Approval rate should rise to ~15-20% (closer to signal quality), deferral rate should drop toward 25%, failure mode shifts from `max_deferrals_reached` to clean reject/approve decisions.

evidence.description: 3.4% approve rate (300/8854) with 95.5% deferral rate suggests triage is risk-averse to approval but unwilling to outright reject. The per-item scoring doesn't use relative context — an idea of score 55 might be top-quartile this week but deferred anyway.

title: Log failure reason granularity for `max_retries_exceeded`
description: 13 `max_retries_exceeded` ideas in the data show no root cause — we can see the cap was hit but not why the retries failed. Without this, we can't distinguish "transient environment" from "idea is genuinely unbuildable" when tuning retry caps (R1).
suggested_change: Extend Metroplex build-outcome schema to include `retry_failure_reasons: [reason_r1, reason_r2]` so the next Sky-Lynx analysis can distinguish transient vs structural retry failures and tune R1 with evidence.
impact: Unblocks data-driven retry-cap tuning in subsequent weeks. Low immediate impact, high meta-value.

evidence.description: 13 `max_retries_exceeded` ideas in the data show no root cause — we can see the cap was hit but not why the retries failed. Without this, we can't distinguish "transient environment" from "idea is genuinely unbuildable" when tuning retry caps (R1).

title: Flag score-compression as a scoring model issue for review
description: Completed-idea scores range 45–62 (range of 17 on a 0-100 scale). Published avg 59.2 vs rejected avg 54.3 — a 4.9-point gap on a compressed scale makes thresholds brittle. IdeaForge weighted scores (0.0–10.0 range, avg 6.1) are spread wider, suggesting the compression is in build-quality scoring, not idea-quality scoring.
suggested_change: Queue a review of the build quality scoring rubric to expand discriminating range — consider adding negative-signal deductions or normalizing to percentile rather than fixed points. Track as a proposal, don't auto-change.
impact: Better score discrimination makes thresholds (R3) and ratchet decisions more stable. Prevents repeated ratchet whiplash.

evidence.description: Completed-idea scores range 45–62 (range of 17 on a 0-100 scale). Published avg 59.2 vs rejected avg 54.3 — a 4.9-point gap on a compressed scale makes thresholds brittle. IdeaForge weighted scores (0.0–10.0 range, avg 6.1) are spread wider, suggesting the compression is in build-quality scoring, not idea-quality scoring.

description: Friction note — "Kup's restart script had hardcoded worktree path that Claude caught and fixed before merge" — indicates tribal knowledge about YCE worktree/path assumptions is being rediscovered per incident, not documented.
suggested_change: Add a short section to `projects/yce-harness/CLAUDE.md` listing preconditions for a healthy YCE dispatch (worktree state, git pull not fetch, agent-id conventions, `-g` / `-y` flags for non-interactive reinstall).
impact: Compounds with R4 (smoke test) — documentation + automation for the same failure class.

evidence.description: Friction note — "Kup's restart script had hardcoded worktree path that Claude caught and fixed before merge" — indicates tribal knowledge about YCE worktree/path assumptions is being rediscovered per incident, not documented.

title: Cap triage deferrals at 5 and force decision
description: 95.5% of triage decisions (8,453/8,855) are `deferred`, and 56/100 completed-idea failures are `max_deferrals_reached(10)`. Current cap is effectively a stalling mechanism, not a decision gate.
suggested_change: Lower Metroplex `max_deferrals` from 10 → 5, and require triage to emit `approve` or `reject` on the 5th evaluation rather than deferring again. Add a "5th-pass forced decision" log line so we can measure downstream impact.
impact: Could halve the time ideas sit in triage and cut the 56-idea deferral-death cohort roughly in half in the next weekly window. Redirects builder capacity to fewer, better-vetted ideas.

evidence.description: 95.5% of triage decisions (8,453/8,855) are `deferred`, and 56/100 completed-idea failures are `max_deferrals_reached(10)`. Current cap is effectively a stalling mechanism, not a decision gate.

title: Enforce quality score threshold of 50 at publish gate
description: Published builds avg 58.3, Tyrest-rejected avg 38.3, review-failed avg 44.0 (n=81). Sky-Lynx's own suggested threshold is 49.8. Currently no threshold is enforced at the gate, so low-quality builds still consume pipeline cycles.
suggested_change: Set Metroplex publish gate to require quality_score ≥ 50 (rounded from 49.8). Route below-threshold builds to a `needs_improvement` bucket rather than auto-publishing or silently failing.
impact: Should eliminate the low-end of the published distribution (n≈0-3 at <50) and give review-failed builds a path back into the loop instead of terminating them.

evidence.description: Published builds avg 58.3, Tyrest-rejected avg 38.3, review-failed avg 44.0 (n=81). Sky-Lynx's own suggested threshold is 49.8. Currently no threshold is enforced at the gate, so low-quality builds still consume pipeline cycles.

title: Reweight routing away from `worker` toward `named` and `forge`
description: `worker` 49% success (75 tasks), `named` 64% (11 tasks), `forge` 100% (2 tasks). Worker carries ~85% of task volume despite being the least reliable. Recent failure: `[worker] Timed out`.
suggested_change: In CMD routing weights, bump `named` priority by ~20% for task types it's demonstrated on, and allow `forge` to pick up 2-3 tasks of the kind currently funneling to `worker`. Keep worker as default for the residual — do not de-route entirely until sample sizes grow.
impact: Plausible ~5-10pt lift in aggregate task success if the reliability differential holds; also builds statistical power on `named`/`forge` to enable larger rebalancing next cycle.

evidence.description: `worker` 49% success (75 tasks), `named` 64% (11 tasks), `forge` 100% (2 tasks). Worker carries ~85% of task volume despite being the least reliable. Recent failure: `[worker] Timed out`.

title: Prune or sponsor the 51 unused skills (14-day gate enforcement)
description: 51/55 deployed skills have zero usage events. Only `prompt-rewriter`, `publish-skill`, `skill-audit`, `skill-maintenance` fire. User's own CLAUDE.md §"14-Day Adoption Gate" is the governing rule.
suggested_change: Run the Sunday tool audit against the full 51, cross-check agent manifests for each, and for any skill with no human-invocation + no agent manifest + no grep-able callsite, delete it. Explicitly protect `get-api-docs` (documented callsite sponsor). Flag `knowledge-compiler`, `failure-postmortem`, `diagnose` for review — their names suggest high potential for Sky-Lynx/Galvatron sponsorship.
impact: Cuts maintenance surface significantly, restores credibility of the gate, and forces the "which agent owns this?" question that drives the cuttlefish insight.

evidence.description: 51/55 deployed skills have zero usage events. Only `prompt-rewriter`, `publish-skill`, `skill-audit`, `skill-maintenance` fire. User's own CLAUDE.md §"14-Day Adoption Gate" is the governing rule.

title: Add a pre-proposal failure-mode confirmation rule to CLAUDE.md
description: `wrong_approach` (14 events) dominates friction. Repeated pattern: Mistral-on-DeepInfra (not hosted), Data bot "SDK version issue" retracted, Claude Desktop hang multi-iteration diagnosis, idea-reality-mcp fix-before-user-revealed-orphan, CMD classification correction. Existing "Diagnostic Approach" rule exists but isn't firing.
suggested_change: Add a one-line reinforcement to `~/CLAUDE.md` Learned Mistakes: *"Before proposing any fix for a bug, infra issue, or failing integration, state the confirmed failure mode in one sentence. If you cannot state it, ask first — do not propose."* Also consider a PreToolUse hook that flags Edit/Write when the preceding user message contains failure keywords but no confirmation turn has occurred.
impact: Could plausibly reduce `wrong_approach` incidents by 30-50% based on the pattern — most of the listed friction entries would have been caught by an explicit failure-mode statement.

evidence.description: `wrong_approach` (14 events) dominates friction. Repeated pattern: Mistral-on-DeepInfra (not hosted), Data bot "SDK version issue" retracted, Claude Desktop hang multi-iteration diagnosis, idea-reality-mcp fix-before-user-revealed-orphan, CMD classification correction. Existing "Diagnostic Approach" rule exists but isn't firing.

title: Starscream — abandon the abstract-thesis opener pattern
description: 20/39 posts at zero engagement, followers flat at 158 for 7+ days. All three listed zero-engagement openers ("The 'dark factory'…", "Most supply chain 'AI' is just…", "Everyone's worried about prompt injection…") share an abstract-contrarian-thesis structure. The top post (5.4% ER) leads with a personal anecdote ("Last Tuesday my build pipeline shipped a feature I never asked for").
suggested_change: Update Starscream's content persona to require the first line of any post to be either (a) a dated personal anecdote or (b) a specific metric from this week's pipeline. Explicitly deprecate abstract-thesis openers of the "Everyone's worried about X while Y…" form.
impact: If engagement lifts to even the 1.2% weekly avg on currently-zero posts, that's ~4-6x aggregate impressions-to-engagement conversion on the bottom half of posts.

title: Investigate preference profile learning-loop stall
description: 166 preferences, avg confidence 0.32, 128 below 0.5, zero changes in the last 7 days despite 733 claude-routed interactions and 6,654 telemetry events. This is inconsistent — if the loop were running, confidence should at least wiggle.
suggested_change: Verify that preference-update jobs ran in the last 7 days (check scheduler logs for the relevant task), confirm the 0.5-confidence promotion path isn't silently failing, and spot-check 2-3 low-confidence preferences against recent sessions to see if evidence exists but isn't being ingested. Do *not* adjust the profile directly until the loop itself is verified working.
impact: High leverage if the loop is broken — currently 77% of preferences are below the actionable threshold. Fixing ingestion likely unlocks meaningful convergence within 2-3 cycles.

evidence.description: 166 preferences, avg confidence 0.32, 128 below 0.5, zero changes in the last 7 days despite 733 claude-routed interactions and 6,654 telemetry events. This is inconsistent — if the loop were running, confidence should at least wiggle.

title: Add triage deduplication guard before build dispatch
description: 2 `triage_rejected: duplicate of already-approved` entries ("AgentGuard", "Capability gap: coding task failed on agent 'worker'") made it deep into the pipeline before being caught. Worker task failures are apparently being auto-translated into ideas without dedup.
suggested_change: Add a pre-build dedup check that compares proposed idea title/summary against the last 90 days of approved and published builds. Reject at triage, not after capacity is spent.
impact: Small-count savings now (2 incidents), but this failure mode scales linearly with pipeline volume and Worker failure rate — worth fixing before the base rate grows.

evidence.description: 2 `triage_rejected: duplicate of already-approved` entries ("AgentGuard", "Capability gap: coding task failed on agent 'worker'") made it deep into the pipeline before being caught. Worker task failures are apparently being auto-translated into ideas without dedup.

title: Shift Sky-Lynx cadence to event-driven on ≥20-session windows
description: Current cadence is Wed + Sun 2 AM semi-weekly. This window captured 50 sessions, which is healthy; but several friction items ("v3 edits got worse", "first Blurr video reverse-motion") are short-horizon and a same-day analysis would catch them while context is warm. L5 roadmap also targets event-driven triggers.
suggested_change: Keep the Wed/Sun baseline but add an event trigger: whenever the rolling window hits 20 sessions OR >10 friction events, run an interim pass. Gate behind dry-run so it doesn't flood proposals.
impact: Tighter feedback loop on systemic friction; risk is report fatigue, which the dry-run gate mitigates.

title: Investigate and likely lower `max_deferrals_reached` cap or tighten deferral criteria
description: 66/100 completed ideas (66%) hit the 10-deferral cap; 8553/8966 triage decisions (95.4%) defer. Deferral is functioning as the default, not the exception.
suggested_change: Consider reducing the deferral cap from 10 → 5, OR tighten the criteria that emit a defer verdict so a larger share resolves to approve/reject on first pass. Run dry-run comparison on last 7 days before committing.
impact: Could recover a meaningful fraction of the 66% deferral-death ideas; reduces triage loop cost (currently 8553 deferrals/period).

evidence.description: 66/100 completed ideas (66%) hit the 10-deferral cap; 8553/8966 triage decisions (95.4%) defer. Deferral is functioning as the default, not the exception.

title: Codify the "confirm failure mode before proposing fix" rule as an active PreToolUse hook
description: `wrong_approach` at 14 events dominates friction (42% of 33 total). Existing `~/CLAUDE.md` rule already calls this out but it is still the #1 friction pattern, suggesting the one-line rule is not enforcing.
suggested_change: Promote the existing textual rule to a PreToolUse hook on Edit/Write that advisory-flags when the preceding user turn contains failure keywords ("broken", "failed", "not working", "doesn't", "isn't") without a subsequent confirmation-of-failure-mode turn. Non-blocking.
impact: Could cut wrong_approach friction by a material fraction; addresses the pattern behind idea-reality-mcp, Data bot, and status-summary miscalls.

evidence.description: `wrong_approach` at 14 events dominates friction (42% of 33 total). Existing `~/CLAUDE.md` rule already calls this out but it is still the #1 friction pattern, suggesting the one-line rule is not enforcing.

title: Validate suggested quality threshold 49.8 against current 47.0 ratchet before accepting
description: Sky-Lynx suggests 49.8 (midpoint of published 58.3 and failed 41.3); ratchet just moved 46.9 → 47.0. Gap of 2.8 points between active ratchet and suggested threshold is unjustified by current data.
suggested_change: Do not auto-adopt 49.8. Hold at 47.0 for one more reactive cycle and re-check whether build success rate ticks above 52% at the new ratchet. If it does, compounding upward is safer than one jump.
impact: Prevents over-correction; preserves published throughput (11 ideas this window).

evidence.description: Sky-Lynx suggests 49.8 (midpoint of published 58.3 and failed 41.3); ratchet just moved 46.9 → 47.0. Gap of 2.8 points between active ratchet and suggested threshold is unjustified by current data.

title: Add rate-limit-aware retry backoff to Metroplex YCE build stage
description: Run 4 contaminated by 163 DeepInfra 429s — external rate limits masked real code-fix signal. 7 ideas hit `max_retries_exceeded: 2` likely inflated by transient 429 responses.
suggested_change: Distinguish 429/5xx from code-level build failures in the retry counter. 429s should trigger exponential backoff without consuming retry budget; only deterministic build failures decrement retries. Cap at 2 remains for real failures.
impact: Reduces false `max_retries_exceeded` events; improves signal-to-noise in failure rate metric; recovers some builds that would otherwise be rejected for external reasons.

evidence.description: Run 4 contaminated by 163 DeepInfra 429s — external rate limits masked real code-fix signal. 7 ideas hit `max_retries_exceeded: 2` likely inflated by transient 429 responses.

title: Move duplicate detection upstream of triage scoring
description: 2 triage_rejected events fired as "duplicate of already-approved" (AgentGuard, coding task capability gap). These ideas consumed triage LLM cost before being rejected on a cheaper check.
suggested_change: Add a pre-triage dedup stage keyed on idea title + normalized summary embedding similarity against `published` + `approved` sets. Reject duplicates before triage.
impact: Small absolute count (2/100) but ~$0 cost to implement; also prevents user-facing confusion when near-duplicates re-appear.

evidence.description: 2 triage_rejected events fired as "duplicate of already-approved" (AgentGuard, coding task capability gap). These ideas consumed triage LLM cost before being rejected on a cheaper check.

title: Tighten Galvatron triage prompt to reduce deferral rate
description: 95.4% deferral rate suggests the triage agent is under-committing. Score compression (avg 54.8, range 45–62) compounds this — scores cluster near the threshold and trip defer logic.
suggested_change: Update Galvatron's triage prompt to force a binary approve/reject decision except when scoring variance is genuinely high (>1.5σ intra-run). Require a confidence band, not just a score. Deferral should be reserved for "need more signal," not "close to threshold."
impact: Could halve deferral volume; improves decision velocity through the funnel.

evidence.description: 95.4% deferral rate suggests the triage agent is under-committing. Score compression (avg 54.8, range 45–62) compounds this — scores cluster near the threshold and trip defer logic.

title: Add outcome-attribution tag distinguishing external-blocker failures from build failures
description: Current friction list lumps "DeepInfra 429", "Netlify GitHub App auth", and "Supabase service role key" with code-level bugs. These are categorically different and should not move the quality ratchet.
suggested_change: Introduce a `failure_cause` tag on build records: `{code_failure, external_api, auth_config, rate_limit, unknown}`. Exclude `external_api` and `rate_limit` from the ratchet input.
impact: More accurate ratchet trajectory; prevents external-world volatility from tightening the internal bar.

evidence.description: Current friction list lumps "DeepInfra 429", "Netlify GitHub App auth", and "Supabase service role key" with code-level bugs. These are categorically different and should not move the quality ratchet.

title: Reduce reactive-trigger sensitivity on ratchet tightening
description: The ratchet moved 46.9 → 47.0 (a 0.1-point change) and triggered this full reactive analysis. Signal-to-compute ratio is low for sub-point moves.
suggested_change: Require ≥0.5-point ratchet movement OR a directional reversal before triggering reactive analysis. Smaller moves can accumulate in the scheduled Wed/Sun run.
impact: Reduces analysis noise without losing meaningful signal; saves LLM cost on low-information triggers.

evidence.description: The ratchet moved 46.9 → 47.0 (a 0.1-point change) and triggered this full reactive analysis. Signal-to-compute ratio is low for sub-point moves.

title: Raise build quality threshold to 49.8 (midpoint between published and failed averages)
description: Published builds avg 58.3 vs rejected avg 38.3 (n=82 scored builds); current threshold permits 38.3-avg rejections to consume build cycles. Suggested threshold 49.8 is derived directly from the data.
suggested_change: Update Metroplex pipeline config to set build quality threshold to 49.8. Gate applied at Tyrest review stage before dispatch to YCE.
impact: Should reduce review_failed (n=9) and tyrest_rejected (n=23) downstream cost by ~30-40%, freeing YCE capacity for higher-score ideas. Published avg may rise from 58.3.

evidence.description: Published builds avg 58.3 vs rejected avg 38.3 (n=82 scored builds); current threshold permits 38.3-avg rejections to consume build cycles. Suggested threshold 49.8 is derived directly from the data.

title: Lower max_deferrals cap from 10 to 5 or add score-decay on deferral
description: 81 of 89 failed ideas (91%) hit `max_deferrals_reached (10)`. 8,703 deferrals vs 302 approvals across lifetime, 520:6 last 7 days. Ideas cycling through 10 deferrals rarely recover — this is a slow bleed, not a queue.

title: Lower Metroplex max_deferrals from 10 to 4
description: 88/100 completed ideas (88%) exited via `max_deferrals_reached (10)`; last 7 days deferred 450 of 502 decisions (89.6%) with only 5 approvals. Each idea consumes 10 triage cycles before being effectively rejected.
suggested_change: Reduce `max_deferrals` cap from 10 → 4 in Metroplex pipeline config. After 4 defers, force a terminal approve/reject decision rather than continuing to defer.
impact: Could cut wasted triage cycles by ~60% (from 8,800 deferrals/100-idea cohort toward ~3,500); forces earlier resolution and surfaces threshold-tuning problems faster.

evidence.description: 88/100 completed ideas (88%) exited via `max_deferrals_reached (10)`; last 7 days deferred 450 of 502 decisions (89.6%) with only 5 approvals. Each idea consumes 10 triage cycles before being effectively rejected.

title: Lower triage approval threshold by ~0.5 weighted-score points
description: 3.3% lifetime approval rate (305/9357); 1.0% last 7d (5/502). Weighted score avg 5.9, problem 6.1, feasibility 6.2 — supply quality looks adequate but conversion is starved. Published builds avg 59.2; rejected avg 53.9 — only ~5pt gap.
suggested_change: Drop the triage approval cutoff by 0.5 weighted points (or equivalent in whatever scoring the gate uses) and observe one cycle. Pair with R1 so deferrals don't absorb the slack.
impact: Should lift weekly approvals from ~5 to ~15-25 without meaningfully degrading published-quality average. Validates whether the bottleneck is supply quality or threshold calibration.

evidence.description: 3.3% lifetime approval rate (305/9357); 1.0% last 7d (5/502). Weighted score avg 5.9, problem 6.1, feasibility 6.2 — supply quality looks adequate but conversion is starved. Published builds avg 59.2; rejected avg 53.9 — only ~5pt gap.

title: Apply suggested quality_threshold=49.8 at the build gate
description: 49% build failure rate (199/409). Published builds avg 58.3 (range 36-60); Build Failed avg 53.0; Tyrest Rejected avg 38.3. The dataset itself surfaces a midpoint of 49.8 between published and failed.
suggested_change: Set Metroplex pre-build quality gate to 49.8 (currently appears below this, given builds at 36-44 are reaching the build stage and failing).
impact: Estimated 30-40% reduction in build failures by filtering the bottom-quality cohort before YCE expends compute; protects publish-rate trend.

title: Deprecate the 49 unused skills that have been deployed >14 days
description: 55/61 skills (90%) recorded zero events this period. The user-level CLAUDE.md "14-Day Adoption Gate" rule explicitly mandates deletion if neither human, agent, nor callsite sponsorship exists. Examples: `aar`, `agent-cost-model`, `bitter-lesson-scorecard`, `boot-tax-monitor`, `compensating-complexity-auditor`, `dark-code-audit`, `failure-asymmetry`, `seedance-prompt`, `vault-setup`, `what-am-i-forgetting`.
suggested_change: Run the Sunday tool audit and, for each unused skill, verify (a) no agent manifest references it and (b) no grep-able callsite in `~/.claude/`, `~/CLAUDE.md`, or active `agent.yaml` files. Delete those that fail all three sponsorship paths. Keep reference skills with documented callsite sponsorship (e.g., `get-api-docs` per the existing rule).
impact: Reduces SessionStart inventory noise, restores signal in skill telemetry, and enforces the rule that already exists but is being violated.

title: Investigate ClaudeClaw preference learning stall
description: 128/166 (77%) preferences below 0.5 confidence, average 0.32, **zero changes in the last 7 days**. Source mix is 137 LLM-discovered vs 29 manual — the LLM-discovered set is dominating with low confidence and not converging.
suggested_change: Audit the preference update job: confirm it's running, check whether confidence-update logic requires an event volume threshold that isn't being hit, and consider purging or re-evaluating the 50+ lowest-confidence LLM-discovered preferences that haven't moved in 30+ days.
impact: Either restores a frozen loop or removes drag from 128 stale low-confidence entries that may be polluting routing/voice decisions downstream.

evidence.description: 128/166 (77%) preferences below 0.5 confidence, average 0.32, **zero changes in the last 7 days**. Source mix is 137 LLM-discovered vs 29 manual — the LLM-discovered set is dominating with low confidence and not converging.

title: Pause or retune Starscream until top-quartile pattern is reproducible
description: 51.3% zero-engagement rate, +0 followers/7d, 0 posts this week, top post 8.7% ER on only 23 impressions (low statistical confidence). The three top performers share concrete-claim openers ("Last Tuesday my build pipeline shipped a feature..."); zero-engagement openers are abstract category claims ("The dark factory isn't science fiction...", "Most supply chain AI is just dashboards...").
suggested_change: Add a Starscream constraint: openers must contain a concrete first-person event, named system, or specific date/number in the first 100 characters. Block posts whose openers match the abstract-category pattern of the zero-engagement set ("Everyone's...", "Most teams...", "The X isn't..."). Reduce posting cadence until the constraint is in place rather than continuing to ship low-engagement content.
impact: Plausibly halves zero-engagement rate based on the observed opener split; lower-confidence due to small N.

title: Route higher-stakes mission tasks away from `worker` toward `named`/`forge`
description: worker 49% success (75 tasks), named 64% (11 tasks), forge 100% (2 tasks). One worker timeout in recent failures. Sample sizes for named/forge are small but worker's 49% on N=75 is statistically meaningful.
suggested_change: Adjust CMD routing weights to bias mission tasks with subtask_complexity ≥ 6 (the upper half of the 1-16 range) toward `named` agents. Keep `worker` as default for low-complexity dispatch. Add a timeout-retry policy that re-routes the second attempt to `named`.
impact: Should lift overall mission success above the current 89% by reducing worker-tier failure cluster; modest given worker still handles volume.

evidence.description: worker 49% success (75 tasks), named 64% (11 tasks), forge 100% (2 tasks). One worker timeout in recent failures. Sample sizes for named/forge are small but worker's 49% on N=75 is statistically meaningful.

title: Deduplicate IdeaForge upstream — 3 of recent rejects were duplicates of already-approved
description: Build outcomes show `triage_rejected: duplicate of already-approved` for AgentGuard, AgentGate MCP Server, and PromptPal CLI. With 362 ideas in the pipeline and 3.3% approval rate, dedupe at ingest is cheaper than at triage.
suggested_change: Add an IdeaForge ingest-time fuzzy-match check against the `published` and `approved` sets (title + 1-line summary embedding similarity ≥ 0.85). Reject duplicates pre-triage instead of consuming a Metroplex slot.
impact: Small absolute (3 cases this period) but a free win; also prevents future drift as the published catalog grows.

evidence.description: Build outcomes show `triage_rejected: duplicate of already-approved` for AgentGuard, AgentGate MCP Server, and PromptPal CLI. With 362 ideas in the pipeline and 3.3% approval rate, dedupe at ingest is cheaper than at triage.

title: Investigate the 5,447,818ms (~91min) latency outlier
description: Telegram completion max latency 5,447,818ms vs avg 149,991ms. One outlier of ~91 minutes is suspicious and roughly matches the documented `/ultraplan` 90-minute timeout window.
suggested_change: Pull the specific event from ClaudeClaw telemetry, confirm whether it's a hung `/ultraplan` session or a different code path. If the former, ensure the existing remote-ultraplan rule in `~/CLAUDE.md` is being applied; if a different path, add a 30-min server-side cancel.
impact: Removes a distortion from the latency average and prevents user-facing 90-min hangs.

evidence.description: Telegram completion max latency 5,447,818ms vs avg 149,991ms. One outlier of ~91 minutes is suspicious and roughly matches the documented `/ultraplan` 90-minute timeout window.

title: Raise quality ratchet to the data-derived midpoint (49.8 → 50)
description: Metroplex computes suggested threshold of 49.8 (midpoint of published avg 58.3 vs failed avg 41.3). Current ratchet is 47.8, admitting builds in the 47.8–49.8 band that historically fail at 41.3 average. All 4 published ideas this week scored ≥36 with avg 60.0 — a 50.0 floor would not exclude any successful build.
suggested_change: Tighten ratchet to 50.0 (round number near the midpoint). Re-evaluate weekly against published-score floor; never raise above min(published_score) for the trailing window.
impact: Should reduce YCE build failures (4 this week) and Tyrest rejections (n=23, avg 38.3) by filtering builds whose scores predict failure. Estimated ~10–15% reduction in failed builds.

title: Diagnose deferral-cap saturation before tuning further quality gates
description: 89/92 rejections (97%) come from `max_deferrals_reached (10)`, not from quality verdicts. 540 deferrals vs 5 approvals in last 7 days (108:1). The deferral mechanism is the rejection mechanism — quality threshold changes will have negligible effect until deferral behavior is understood.
suggested_change: Add a Metroplex query that breaks down *why* ideas defer (which gate, which condition). Surface this in next Sky-Lynx run before recommending any further threshold change. Consider whether the deferral cap of 10 is appropriate or if ideas should fail-fast at lower deferral counts with clearer reasons.
impact: Unblocks evidence-based tuning of the dominant failure mode (96% of all failures). Without this, all other pipeline tuning is operating on the long tail.

title: Promote duplicate detection earlier in the triage flow
description: 3 ideas this week (AgentGuard, AgentGate MCP Server, PromptPal CLI) reached triage and were rejected as duplicates of already-approved ideas. These wasted triage cycles and likely accumulated deferrals before rejection.
suggested_change: Move duplicate detection to the IdeaForge ingestion stage (before triage) — cross-check new ideas against the already-approved set on title/embedding similarity. Reject or merge at ingest, not at triage.
impact: Removes ~3 duplicate-rejections/week from triage load and prevents deferral-cycle waste on dead-end ideas. Modest but compounding.

evidence.description: 3 ideas this week (AgentGuard, AgentGate MCP Server, PromptPal CLI) reached triage and were rejected as duplicates of already-approved ideas. These wasted triage cycles and likely accumulated deferrals before rejection.

title: Add YCE-build-failure root-cause logging to Metroplex telemetry
description: 4 YCE build failures this week (265-r1, 277-r1, 285, 282) — two with `-r1` retry suffix indicating retry-and-still-fail. No failure category breakdown available in current telemetry. Build pipeline overall is at 50.9% completion with 199 failures — root causes are opaque.
suggested_change: Extend Metroplex build records with a `failure_category` field (e.g., dependency_install, test_failure, lint_failure, timeout, runtime_error). Surface category breakdown in Sky-Lynx pipeline reader.
impact: Enables targeted recommendations on the 199-failure cohort instead of treating it as a black box. High leverage given the volume.

title: Add a "score floor" guard separate from the ratchet (hard reject < 45)
description: Tyrest rejected scores range 36.0–56.0 (avg 38.3, n=23). Review Failed scores range 26.0–60.0 (avg 44.0, n=9). The lower-bound builds (26–40) are extremely unlikely to ever publish — published cohort minimum is 36 but average 58.3.
suggested_change: Add a static floor (e.g., 45) below which ideas are rejected before consuming a build slot, independent of the moving ratchet. The ratchet handles the marginal band; the floor handles the obvious-no band.
impact: Frees build capacity for marginal ideas that have a real chance. Estimated savings: ~8–10 build slots/week based on Tyrest+Review-Failed score distribution.

evidence.description: Tyrest rejected scores range 36.0–56.0 (avg 38.3, n=23). Review Failed scores range 26.0–60.0 (avg 44.0, n=9). The lower-bound builds (26–40) are extremely unlikely to ever publish — published cohort minimum is 36 but average 58.3.

title: Increase weight on Python+MCP-SDK pattern in build planning
description: Project CLAUDE.md already documents "Python + MCP SDK is the proven tech stack for MCP servers (100% success rate across completed builds)". 4 published builds this week with avg score 60.0 are consistent with this pattern. Failed YCE builds and lower-quality rejections may be deviating from the proven stack.
suggested_change: In Galvatron's build-plan generation, add an explicit preference: when an idea is classified `tool` and involves MCP, default to Python+MCP-SDK unless an explicit reason to deviate is documented in the plan. Surface deviations in the build record.
impact: Reinforces a known-good pattern across the 264 `tool`-classified ideas in the queue (71% of all ideas). Could lift completion rate above 50.9%.

title: Reconsider Sky-Lynx schedule in light of reactive triggers
description: This run is reactive (quality ratchet tightened) on 2026-04-30, outside the Wed/Sun cron. Total Sessions = 0 for the period suggests interactive-session metrics are no longer the primary input — pipeline metrics dominate.
suggested_change: Consider gating the scheduled Wed run on "have any of {ratchet changed, build_failed > N, deferral_rate spiked}" — skip the run if nothing has materially changed since the last analysis. Keep Sun as the unconditional baseline.
impact: Reduces redundant low-information reports; sharpens signal-to-noise on the reports that do fire.

title: Raise the quality ratchet from 47.8 to 49.8 to match the data-suggested midpoint
description: Suggested threshold is 49.8 (midpoint of published avg 58.3 and failed avg 41.3); current ratchet at 47.8 is 2.0 points below this. Tyrest-rejected builds average 38.3 and Review-Failed average 44.0 — both well under 49.8 — while published builds average 58.3.
suggested_change: Bump the ratchet to 49.8 in one step (the data already supports it; staging adds latency without de-risking).
impact: Should reduce downstream Tyrest/Review rejections and raise build-success rate above the current 50.4%. Conservatively, expect a 5–10 percentage-point lift in completed/published ratio.

title: Cap deferrals at 5 (down from 10) to stop the deferral-exhaustion outcome path
description: 89% of completed ideas this week exited via `max_deferrals_reached (10)`. Of 9490 lifetime triage decisions, 9023 (95.1%) are deferrals. The deferral mechanism is functioning as a slow-rejection path rather than a "wait for more signal" mechanism.
suggested_change: Reduce `max_deferrals` from 10 → 5 and require triage to commit to approve/reject earlier.
impact: Frees throughput, reduces zombie ideas in queue, surfaces decision pressure on triage. Expect deferral count to drop ~50% with negligible effect on approval rate (since most deferrals end in rejection anyway).

title: Add a pre-triage duplicate check against already-approved ideas
description: 3 of the week's triage rejections (AgentGuard, AgentGate MCP Server, PromptPal CLI) were "duplicate of already-approved" — duplicate detection is happening at triage rather than at intake, wasting triage cycles.
suggested_change: Add a name+embedding similarity check at IdeaForge intake against the approved-idea registry; auto-classify matches as `duplicate` before they enter the triage queue.
impact: Eliminates a recurring waste category at triage. Low absolute volume (3/week), but the pattern suggests it grows as the approved registry grows.

evidence.description: 3 of the week's triage rejections (AgentGuard, AgentGate MCP Server, PromptPal CLI) were "duplicate of already-approved" — duplicate detection is happening at triage rather than at intake, wasting triage cycles.

title: Investigate the priority queue's 63% failure rate before promoting more work into it
description: Priority queue shows 73 failed vs 43 completed (63% failure) — materially worse than the general pipeline's 50.4% success rate. This is the opposite of what a "priority" queue should produce.
suggested_change: Pause new priority-queue dispatches for a short audit window (24–48h), pull the 73 failure records, and identify whether failures cluster around a specific build template, retry policy, or score band.
impact: If the failure cluster is identified, fixing it lifts the pipeline's overall success rate disproportionately because priority items are dispatched first.

evidence.description: Priority queue shows 73 failed vs 43 completed (63% failure) — materially worse than the general pipeline's 50.4% success rate. This is the opposite of what a "priority" queue should produce.

title: Add a retry-budget cap on YCE builds and inspect the `-r1` failure cluster
description: Two of this week's four named YCE failures (`metroplex-ideaforge-265-r1`, `277-r1`) are `-r1` retries that still failed. The pipeline appears to retry without learning, burning compute on the same failure mode.
suggested_change: Cap automatic retries at 1, and require the second retry to include a diagnostic delta (changed prompt, changed template, or human flag). Log the failure mode of each `-r0`/`-r1` pair to identify whether retries ever change outcome.
impact: Reduces wasted YCE build compute; may surface a systemic build-template defect.

evidence.description: Two of this week's four named YCE failures (`metroplex-ideaforge-265-r1`, `277-r1`) are `-r1` retries that still failed. The pipeline appears to retry without learning, burning compute on the same failure mode.

title: Tighten Tyrest review criteria — current rejected avg (38.3) is 9.5 points below published avg
description: Tyrest rejected 23 builds this week with an average score of 38.3 (range 36.0–56.0). The 56.0 upper bound overlaps with published builds (36.0–60.0), suggesting Tyrest is rejecting some work that scores in the publishable band.
suggested_change: Audit the 5–10 highest-scoring Tyrest-rejected builds against the lowest-scoring published builds. If Tyrest is over-rejecting at the boundary, recalibrate; if under-rejecting at the bottom, document the criteria.
impact: Improves consistency between scoring and the Tyrest verdict; could recover salvageable builds currently being discarded.

evidence.description: Tyrest rejected 23 builds this week with an average score of 38.3 (range 36.0–56.0). The 56.0 upper bound overlaps with published builds (36.0–60.0), suggesting Tyrest is rejecting some work that scores in the publishable band.

title: Schedule a weekly Metroplex calibration check that auto-suggests the next ratchet value
description: This week's data already produced a clean suggested threshold (49.8). The signal exists; what's missing is automation to surface it on a cadence rather than relying on reactive Sky-Lynx triggers.
suggested_change: Add a weekly scheduled task (Sunday) that recomputes the published/failed midpoint and posts a one-line proposal to `sky_lynx/data/proposals.db` if the suggested threshold differs from the current ratchet by ≥1.0.
impact: Makes ratchet calibration continuous and low-friction; reduces drift between gate value and observed outcome distribution.

evidence.description: This week's data already produced a clean suggested threshold (49.8). The signal exists; what's missing is automation to surface it on a cadence rather than relying on reactive Sky-Lynx triggers.

title: Track score-band compression as a quality-input signal
description: Completed ideas this week scored 45–62 (a 17-point band) with average 54.0. Build quality scores ranged 26–60 with average 50.9. The narrow upper bound suggests scoring rubrics may be ceiling-capped or that input ideas cluster in a similar-quality band.
suggested_change: Add a weekly metric: standard deviation of scores by stage. If σ stays below ~6 across consecutive weeks, escalate to a scoring-rubric review.
impact: Exploratory — a flat distribution limits the discriminating power of any threshold-based gate. This is a measurement, not a fix.
